title: Single-gene short-term CRISPR ko viability assay
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"3r5fq","text":"The Cancer Dependency Map Project is using genome-scale CRISPR loss-of-function screens on a large number of  cancer models to systematically identify selective genetic dependencies, which in turn represent potential therapeutic targets. As we identify potential targets through this project, it is becoming increasingly important to have a robust validation strategy to de-risk individual targets before embarking on costly therapeutic projects. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":34}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8t5eh","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8s29i","text":"Here we describe an experimental strategy for assessing the short-term viability effect after knocking out a gene of interest via CRISPR/Cas9 using a commercially-available luminescent cell viability assay reagent (Cell-titer Glo [Promega]) in a 96-well format.  The robustness and reproducibility of this assay are critically dependent on two experimental parameters: seeding density of the cells and the lentivirus infection efficiency. We provide standardized protocols for the rigorous determination of both of these parameters. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":59,"length":29}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7ldtl","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"16c80","text":"In addition, given that CRISPR-mediated gene knockout penetrance differ across cell lines, the use of standardized controls is necessary for the comparative assessment of the strength of the dependency across cell lines. Moreover, there is a non-specific toxicity effect resulting from the DNA double-strand breaks caused by the CRISPR/Cas9 system. It is therefore important to use sgRNAs targeting intragenic regions, which serve as negative controls to account for this non-specific toxicity. We have tested and identified the sequences of negative and positive sgRNAs controls (pan-essential genes) that can be used to scale the cell viability read-outs. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"tged","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"amsij","text":"Finally, we also provide a series of templates to record and normalize the data produced throughout this assay. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8nf3r","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"d72ee","text":"Our protocol for the cell-titer-glo assay used to quantify the viability of your cells is nearly the same as that of the provided Promega CTG kit (ref# G7573). ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":147,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ejrpi","text":"One notable exception is that we use 25ul of undiluted CTG reagent per 100ul of media in 96 well format.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"72mvt","text":"Additionally, we recommend changing the media to 100ul per well for each plate that will be read out. This will ensure uniformity of CTG density as media evaporates at variables rates across different wells.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"5mb3b","text":"Obtain and propagate 5+ models (i.e., cell lines) that are putatively sensitive on the genetic perturbation to be tested as well as 5+ lineage-matched control models that are supposed to be insensitive to the same perturbation","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ej2r6","text":"(Optional) Adapt all the cell line models to growth in RPMI1640 medium supplemented with 10% fetal bovine serum (FBS) ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"9rud9","text":"Use BL2+ facility for lentivirus infection and subsequent propagation of infected cells.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: (Day 0) Template/Planning : create a copy of the "Viability Assay Optimization" template and fill out the information.

(Day 0) Make Viral Master Plate : prepare (a) master plate(s) of BRD003 lenti-virus that contains the following volume of virus per 40 uL of total volume %s %s * NOTE- up to 4 cell types/one master plate; if seeding > 4 cell types, make multiple master plates
Prepare (# of cell types) x 400 uL of BRD003 virus
Fill column 2, rows B-G and columns 3-11, rows B-F (wells w. black numbers) with (# of cell types) x 50 uL of R10 medium
Aliquot (# of cell types) x 100 uL of BRD003 virus into columns 3-11, row G (wells w. red numbers)
Transfer (# of cell types) x 50 uL of virus-containing medium from columns 3-11, row G to columns 3-11, row F and mix well (not well itself but its content); change chips of multichannel pipetter
Transfer (# of cell types) x 50 uL of virus-containing medium from columns 3-11, row F to columns 3-11, row E and mix well (not well itself but its content); change chips of multichannel pipetter
Transfer (# of cell types) x 50 uL of virus-containing medium from columns 3-11, row C to columns 3-11, row B and mix well (not well itself but its content)
(Day 0) Cell Seeding : seed the numbers of cells in 100 uL medium (containing 5.6 ug/mL polybrebne [H9268, SIgma-Aldrich]) indicated in this table in two 96 well plates (per cell line): %s %s %s %s
make 6 x (# of cell type) mL medium containing 5.6 ug/mL polybrene (this is ‘A’).
make a suspension of 40K cell/10 mL medium (w/ 5.6 ug/mL polybrene) (this is ‘B’).
Aliquot 75 uL of A into Plate I, columns 2-8 .
Aliquot 50 uL of A into Plate I, columns 9-11; Plate II, columns 2-5 (except for 2E, 2F, 2G).
Aliquot 25 uL of B into Plate I, columns 2-8.
Aliquot 50 uL of B into Plate I, columns 9-11; Plate II, columns 2-5 (except for 2E, 2F, 2G).
Aliquot 100 uL of B into Plate II, columns 6-11 and 2E, 2F, 2G.
(Day 0) Infection : transfer 40 uL of (virus-containing) medium from the virus master plate to the corresponding well of cell-seeded plate; centrifuge at 900 x g, 1.5h at 37°C.
(Day 1) Medium change/Puro selection: change medium with 200 uL of fresh medium- Puro (+): For only Plate 1, columns 6-8 and Plate II, columns 3-5, 9-11, use 2 ug/mL Puromycin-containing medium.
(Day 3) change medium (Puro (-) or (+)).
(Day 5) change medium (Puro (-) or (+)).
(Day 7) CTG measurement : Perform the CellTiter-Glo protocol provided by Promega and paste the CTG values for each cell line under the "Assay Dev" tab of the "Viability Assay Optimization" template.

For each of the seeding densities – i.e., 1000, 2000, 4000 cells/well --, determine the minimum volume of virus that yields (mean CTG value of Puro (+) wells) > 0.8 x (mean CTG value of Puro (-) wells): Set the mean Puro (-) CTG value for this volume of virus as C 1000 (or C 2000 , C 4000 ); check if the C value determined here is larger than ‘0.7 x (corresponding mean CTG value of infection/Puro (-) sample)’.
If C 4000 ≥ 1.8 x C 2000 , use 4000 cells/well (if not, go to 11).
If 1.5 x C 2000 ≤ C 4000 < 1.8 x C 2000 , use 3000 cell//well (if not, go to 12).
If C 2000 ≥ 1.8 x C 1000 , use 2000 cells/well (if not, go to 13).

Determine the minimum volume of virus that gives (mean CTG value of Puro (+) wells) > 0.8 x (mean CTG value of Puro (-) wells) for the detemined seeding density of corresponding cells. If an interpolated number of cells is used (3000 or 1500 cells/ well), take the mean virus volume for the "4000" and "2000" values. Based on this virus volume (which reflects sensitivity to viral infection/viral toxicity), categorize cell lines used for a single project (usually around 10 cell lines of varying lineages) into 3 classes : HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW infection sensitivity. When you prepare a set of virus for viability assay, pick one cell line from each class and titrate all the virus against these cell lines (see an example below): %s %s

Measure the titers of 9 types of virus prepared/aliquoted as below, using 3 representative cells lines -- 1 from HIGH, MEDIUM, and LOW infection sensitivity (as identified by the Week1 'Assay Dev' [above].

Prepare virus for each of the: 3 negative controls (BRD003-sgLacZ/-sgCH2-2/-sgAAVS1) 3 positive controls (BRD003-sgPOLR2D/sgSF3B1/-sgKIF11) 3 target-specific guides (BRD-003-sgXXX-A/-sgXXX-B/-sgXXX-C) Prepare virus in 1x15cm dish; this will yield approx 17 mL of virus. Aliquot the virus into 1.5 mLx11 vials and assemble as below: %s %s sgRNA sequence for all controls: %s %s
Pick 3 cell lines representing HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW infection sensitivity based on the ‘Assay Development’ result e.g. SNU503( LOW ), S W837 ( MEDIUM ), HCC202( HIGH )
(Day 1) Change medium (Plate A: just medium; Plate B: medium with 2 uG/uL Puro)
(Day 4) Read CTG
Determine appropriate amount of virus to add per cell line
Plot the titration result (CTG value; see below)
Determine the amount of virus (X) that fulfills both: A) CTG ​ virus=x, Puro(-) / CTG​ virus=0, Puro(-) ≥​0.8​, B) ​CTG ​ virus=x, Puro (+) / CTG​ virus=0, Puro(-) ≥​0.8
When multiple virus amounts fulfill the above criteria, pick the amount of virus that gave the largest CTG Puro(+) value. This will usually match the peak of the Puro (+) CTG curve.
Make a table (see below) and use this as a reference for the amount of virus to be used for cell lines with HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW infection sensitivity: %s %s

Before starting the assy, you should know: Seeding Density and Infection Sensitivity (for each cell line tested) and Viral Titer (for each virus tested). (Day 0) Create a copy of the "CTG normalization template" and fill out the information (there are 6 lines per template; if testing > 6 cell lines, create multiple sheets).
Cell seeding; seed # of cells in accordance with the result from the seeding density measurement; One 96-well plate (rows B-G; columns 1-11).
(Day 1) Infection
Dilute Virus with 10% FBS RPMI; use the "Virus Calculation for CTG Assay" template to calculate how much virus/medium to be mixed.
Aliquot 325 uL of diluted virus to single row (columns 1-11)/cell line of the Master Plate(s). %s %s
Replace the medium of cell-seeded plates with 50uL of 10% FBS RPMI containing 8ug/mL polybrene.
Add 50 uL of virus from Master Plate to corresponding wells (in rows B-G) of cell-seeded plate.
Spin plate at 900 x g for 1.5 hours at 37C.
(for sensitive cells) replace medium with 100uL of fresh cell-specific medium right after spinning.
(Day 2) Selection : Replace medium with 100 uL of fresh medium [for antibiotics (-)] or antibiotics-containing medium [for antibiotics (+)] . %s %s
(Day 5) Medium change : Replace medium with 100 uL of fresh medium [for antibiotics (-)] or antibiotics-containing medium [for antibiotics (+)]
(Day 7) CTG Assay

Final CTG values from a plate reader can be copied and pasted directly into "CTG Normalization Template. The results for the final assay will be normalized such that 0 is equal to an average of our negative (cutting) controls and -1 is equal to the average of our positive controls.
(Day 0) Seeding : Prepare 15ml of cell suspension with: *[# of cells to seed per well] x 150 cells in R10, containing 10ul f 8 uG/uL polybrene
Seed 100 uL/well into two 96-well plates per cell line: %s %s %s %s

Make virus 'Master Plate': Create a master plate (enough virus for both seeded plates) by serial diluting pure virus in R10 with a multichannel pipette: %s %s Fill row G (columns 3-11) with indicated amount of virus. Fill rows B-F (columns 3-11) with indicated amount of medium (eg. R10). Fill rows B-F (columns 3-11) with indicated amount of medium (eg. R10). Transfer half the volume from row G to row F and mix well. transfer half the volume from row F to row E and mix well. Transfer half the volume from row E to row D and mix well. Transfer half the volume from row D to row C and mix well. Transfer half the volume from row C to row B and mix well.
(Day 0) Infection : Transfer 40 uL of virus to cell-seeded plates form the corresponding well in Master Plate. Centrifuge at 900 x g, 37-degree for 1.5h.
Replace medium with 100 uL/well of warm 10% FBS RPMI
Add 25 uL/well of CellTiter-Glo Reagent (Promega, G7573)
Shake on a plate shaker for 30 min RT
Read luminescence using a plate reader (eg., EnVision, PerkinElmer, #2105-0010)
creator: Tsukasa Shibue
